title: Unable to reach container from another host in same network
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I use wg0 interface between two hosts, and trying to reach container that is exposed on one of hosts from another host.
Container that is started with podman is reachable from same host, but not reachable from another host on same network.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. wg0 interface is preconfigured.
host 10.66.66.1 - host 1 (server, runs podman)
host 10.66.66.6 - host 2 (client)

2. on host 1 run `podman run -p 10.66.66.1:8000:80 nginxdemos/hello`
3. on host 2 run `curl -v http://10.66.66.1:8000`

**Describe the results you received:**

Example message of error:
```
* connect to 10.66.66.1 port 8000 failed: Connection timed out
* Failed to connect to 10.66.66.1 port 8000: Connection timed out
* Closing connection 0
curl: (28) Failed to connect to 10.66.66.1 port 8000: Connection timed out
```

UFW/iptables message in logs:
```
[UFW BLOCK] IN=wg0 OUT=cni-podman0 MAC= SRC=10.66.66.6 DST=10.88.2.16 LEN=60 TOS=0x00 PREC=0x00 TTL=63 ID=48180 DF PROTO=TCP SPT=56606 DPT=80 WINDOW=64860 RES=0x00 SYN URGP=0 
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Successful reply from nginx server.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
The host 1 is perfectly reachable from host 2 if I host any other hello-world application on same port (directly on it, not via podman).
Using `--network=host` also helps to make host reachable like mentioned in [another ticket](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/8814) but in there it was due to conflicting docker firewall rules (which I have never installed on my host)

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.14
Built:        Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

   Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
Listing... Done
podman/unknown,now 2.2.1~4 amd64 [installed]
podman/unknown 2.2.1~4 arm64
podman/unknown 2.2.1~4 armhf
podman/unknown 2.2.1~4 ppc64el
podman/unknown 2.2.1~4 s390x
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Its tested on VPS on Debian 10 with podman installed from Kubic repo.
The system is using `iptables` via `UFW` with few rules to allow connections coming from `wg0` interface (wireguard).
The obvious guess is something is going wrong with iptables rules, but why, since its going to proper `cni-podman0` (that is automatically created by podman anyway) ? Multiple "from scratch" tests and reboots - still same effect.

comments.body:
Figured, iptables as guessed, this is the rule that it required:
```
ufw route allow in on wg0 out on cni-podman0
```
